disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden, compelling urge to void that is very hard to defer, with occasional leakage occurring before reaching the toilet
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe exactly what happens when you need to go?
Do you ever leak before you get to the toilet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It comes on all of a sudden and I can barely hold it — sometimes a bit leaks out before I even make it to the toilet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Markedly increased daytime frequency (>15 voids/day) and near-hourly nocturia, each void small in volume
trigger_question_examples: How many times are you passing urine day and night?
How much comes out each time?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: More than fifteen times a day and up almost every hour at night, but only tiny amounts each time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No dysuria, no visible haematuria, and no flank or back pain (pertinent negatives)
trigger_question_examples: Is there any burning or stinging when you pass urine?
Have you seen any blood, or any pain in your back or sides?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no burning at all, I haven't seen any blood, and there's no pain in my back or sides.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Normal urinary stream with no hesitancy or straining; the patient is able to pass urine (no obstruction or inability to void)
trigger_question_examples: Is your stream normal, or are you having to strain or wait for it to start?
Are you able to actually empty, or does it feel like you can't pass anything?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The stream's normal — I don't strain and it starts fine. I can definitely pass urine, it's the constant urgency that's the problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heavy recent caffeine and energy-drink intake used to counter sleep deprivation
trigger_question_examples: How much caffeine or how many energy drinks are you having?
Has anything in your diet or drinks changed lately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been downing coffee and energy drinks all day to stay awake through the exhaustion.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe sleep deprivation, distress, social withdrawal, and a self-identified falls risk from rushing to the toilet while exhausted
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting your sleep and daily life?
Are you worried about falling when you rush at night?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I haven't slept in three days, I'm too scared to leave the house, and I nearly go over rushing to the loo at night when I'm this tired.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Screening negatives for emergency features — no fever or rigors, no complete inability to pass urine, no lower abdominal distension or agonising bladder pain
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any fever, shivering or feel unwell in yourself?
Has there been any point where you completely couldn't pass urine, or a painful swollen lower tummy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or shivers, and no — I've never been completely unable to go; my lower tummy isn't swollen or painful, it's just the constant urgency.
